topic.name: U.S. Legislative Designations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts that involve the designation of geographical locations and facilities, as well as amendments to federal statutes.

Document 1: directs the United States Board on Geographic Names to designate a specified unnamed peak
designate a specified icefield in the northern Chugach National Forest
Document 2: designates the Department of Veterans Affairs community based outpatient clinic in Mansfield, Ohio
as the 'David F. Winder Department of Veterans Affairs Community Based Outpatient Clinic'
Document 3: amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968
creates a new term of service for the office of Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code affecting tax deductions and filing processes.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend the period in which a corporation may carryover excess contributions
tax deduction for charitable contributions from 5 to 20 succeeding taxable years
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to enable individual taxpayers to file their income tax returns directly
through the Internal Revenue Service website without the use of any intermediary
Document 3: amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the 15-year recovery period for qualified leasehold improvement property
for purposes of the tax deduction for depreciation

topic.name: Education Grants and Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at providing funding and support for educational initiatives, including restorative justice, afterschool programs, and cybersecurity education.

Document 1: Restorative Justice in Schools Act of 2011
train school personnel in restorative justice and conflict resolution
Document 2: Afterschool Partnerships Improve Results in Education (ASPIRE) Act
competitive matching grants to partnerships for the development of national model afterschool interdisciplinary education programs
Document 3: Cybersecurity Education Enhancement Act of 2008
awarding competitive grants to institutions of higher education for cybersecurity professional development programs

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to Medicare and Medicaid provisions under the Social Security Act.

Document 1: Amends part B (Supplementary Medical Insurance) of title XVIII (Medicare)
limit the penalty for late enrollment under part B
Document 2: amends part A (General Provisions) of title XI of the Social Security Act
authorize the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to waive applicable general and Medicaid requirements
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to extend through April 1, 2009
approval of specified waivers under title XIX (Medicaid)

topic.name: Tariff and Trade Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments related to the suspension of duties on specific goods and services within the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend temporarily the suspension of duty on certain sector mold press machines.
suspension of duty on certain sector mold press machines.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on reusable surgical drapes of textile materials.
suspend temporarily the duty on reusable surgical drapes of textile materials.
Document 3: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to suspend the limitation on the period for which borrowers are eligible for loan guarantee assistance through December 31, 2013.
suspend the limitation on the period for which borrowers are eligible for loan guarantee assistance.

topic.name: Veterans Affairs Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving services and support for veterans in areas such as healthcare, housing, and energy projects.

Document 1: Prohibits the appropriation of funds... for any VA alternative energy generation project
Directs the VA to transfer all unobligated funds... for veterans' medical care.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development... to establish a pilot program to award grants to nonprofit organizations
Requires such grants to be used to rehabilitate and modify the primary residence of disabled or low-income veterans.
Document 3: This bill directs the Department of Defense (DOD) and the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to jointly establish... a uniform formulary
a uniform formulary for DOD and VA that includes all appropriate systemic pain and psychotropic drugs.

topic.name: Foster Care and Adoption Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving foster care and adoption systems in the United States, particularly focusing on interstate placements and assistance for children in these systems.

Document 1: require the procedures a state must have in effect for the orderly and timely interstate placement of children
authorize grants for development of an electronic interstate case-processing system
Document 2: Amends part E (Foster Care and Adoption Assistance) of SSA title XI
increase the federal medical assistance percentage (FMAP) for foster care and adoption assistance in such territories
Document 3: in the event of the death or incapacity of the relative guardian, the eligibility of a child for a kinship guardian assistance payment shall not be affected

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on consumer protections related to financial transactions and disclosures.

Document 1: Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act with respect to mandatory fee disclosures on automated teller machines (ATMs) operated by a person other than a financial institution holding a consumer's account.
Repeals the requirement that such a fee disclosure appear in a prominent and conspicuous location on or at the ATM.
Document 2: Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act with respect to mandatory fee disclosures on automated teller machines (ATMs) operated by a person other than a financial institution holding a consumer's account.
Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to make the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) a 'covered agency' that may share information with another covered agency or any other federal agency.

topic.name: Public Land Conveyance Acts
topic.description: Legislation facilitating the transfer and management of public lands for various purposes, including conservation and recreation.

Document 1: Grants the city of Mesquite, Nevada, the exclusive right to purchase certain parcels of public land
Requires proceeds from certain sales to be used for the implementation of the Virgin River Multispecies Habitat Conservation Plan.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of the Interior to convey specified federal lands
Requires the Connell Lake conveyance area to be managed in accordance with the terms of the Recreation and Public Purposes Act.
Document 3: Authorizes the Utah National Parks Council of the Boy Scouts of America to convey to Brian Head Resort certain public land
for the purpose of operating a camp in exchange for conveyance by Brian Head Resort of certain of its private land to the Boy Scouts.

topic.name: Political Transparency Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing transparency and accountability in political processes, including candidate disclosures and the use of public funds.

Document 1: require each candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) a copy of the candidate's income tax returns
shall be treated in the same manner as a report filed by the candidate
Document 2: prohibits the use by a recipient or its subsidiary of Troubled Asset Relief Program (TARP) funds under the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008 for lobbying expenditures or political contributions
Requires the Secretary to make such reports and certifications publicly available online and free of charge
Document 3: Directs the Federal Trade Commission to revise the do-not-call registry provisions
prohibits politically-oriented recorded message telephone calls to telephone numbers listed on that registry

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures related to immigration and the adjustment of status for specific groups within the United States.

Document 1: adjust to permanent resident status a qualifying Liberian national
must be filed not later than one year after the date of enactment
Document 2: permit a lawful alien who enters the United States on a nonimmigrant student visa
reimburses the local educational agency for the full, unsubsidized per capita cost
Document 3: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to permit multiple employers to submit a joint petition
application for workers with staggered start and end dates in the same application

topic.name: U.S. National Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to national security, intelligence operations, and military affairs in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the President to develop and transmit to the appropriate congressional committees, with intelligence support from the Director of National Intelligence, a comprehensive interagency strategy and implementation plan for long-term security and stability in Afghanistan.
Authorizes appropriations for Afghanistan.
Document 2: Amends the National Security Act of 1947 to direct the head of an element of the intelligence community (IC) to provide to the Secretary of Defense any intelligence information obtained by such IC element.
Requires the Secretary to report annually to Congress concerning the instances in which such information was provided and the response to the information.
Document 3: Revises the definition of 'entitlement land' for which the Secretary of the Interior is required to make payments to local governments in lieu of taxes.
Includes federal land under the jurisdiction of the Secretary of Defense (DOD) or the Secretary of a military department.

topic.name: FDA Drug and Device Approval Processes
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at streamlining and enhancing the approval processes for medical drugs and devices by the FDA, particularly for pediatric and rare disease treatments.

Document 1: establish a reciprocal marketing approval process
the FDA must grant or decline reciprocal marketing approval
Document 2: require an application for the approval of a medical device
increasing pediatric access to medical devices
Document 3: permit the Food and Drug Administration to allow the sponsor of a new drug
rely upon information submitted for an approved medication

topic.name: Early Childhood and Trauma Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation focused on improving early childhood education and addressing trauma in children, highlighting the importance of professional development for educators and support services for affected youth.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Education and the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to award competitive matching grants to states to support services for early childhood educators
Requires grant applicants to demonstrate that they have a comprehensive early childhood educator professional development system or a plan for developing one.
Document 2: This bill amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to support programs that provide for the continued operation of the National Child Traumatic Stress Initiative
focus on the development of services and resources to prevent the long-term consequences of child trauma.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Support
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at supporting various aspects of agriculture, including crop production, pricing, and rural housing assistance.

Document 1: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 to consider mung beans and pulse crops as covered commodities
producers on a farm may reduce the base acres for any covered commodity for a crop year by one acre for each acre used for the production of fruits or vegetables
Document 2: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to authorize the Secretary of Agriculture to consider national average market price variations for different classes of wheat
determining wheat producer eligibility for 2007 counter-cyclical payments
Document 3: Amends the Housing Act of 1949 to revise the requirements for consideration as rural areas with respect to assistance for farm housing
continue to be so classified until the receipt of data from the decennial census in the year 2020

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Security Assistance
topic.description: The documents discuss U.S. legislative measures aimed at promoting peace, providing security assistance, and reviewing nuclear policy in relation to international conflicts.

Document 1: recognizes that there has been a failure of leadership in South Sudan
urges all parties in the conflict to cease all violence
Document 2: authorizes the President to provide Ukraine with specified defense articles
increases military-to-military interactions of the U.S. Armed Forces with the armed forces of Ukraine
Document 3: directs the President to conduct a nuclear policy review
requires the Secretary of Defense to conduct a comprehensive review of the U.S. nuclear posture

topic.name: Social Security and Disability Benefits
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments related to eligibility and benefits for social security and disability programs.

Document 1: reducing from 10 to five the number of years of marriage prior to divorce required for a divorced spouse to be eligible for benefits
OASDI program
Document 2: amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974
permit the plan sponsor of a multiemployer pension plan to amortize net investment losses
Document 3: provides for payment of disability benefits to public safety officers
who became permanently and totally disabled prior to the enactment of such Act

topic.name: Transportation Infrastructure Funding
topic.description: The documents discuss legislative measures related to funding and regulations for transportation infrastructure, particularly focusing on rail and highway projects in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Safe, Accountable, Flexible, Efficient Transportation Equity Act
eligible for use of a state's apportionment of coordinated border infrastructure program funds
Document 2: eliminates certain grounds for waiving Buy American requirements
authorizes the Secretary to waive such requirements if the cost of U.S.-produced components is more than 60%

Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to make capital grants to a class I, class II, or class III railroad
prescribes prevailing wage rate requirements for such projects in accordance with the Davis-Bacon Act.

topic.name: Fisheries Management and Conservation
topic.description: The documents discuss U.S. legislation aimed at enhancing fisheries management and conservation efforts, particularly concerning sharks and maritime territorial disputes.

Document 1: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act
funding of Council research and monitoring priorities
Document 2: Amends the High Seas Driftnet Fishing Moratorium Protection Act
requires the Secretary to seek to enter into international shark conservation agreements
Document 3: reaffirm U.S. support for the peaceful resolution of maritime territorial disputes
support U.S. Armed Forces operations in support of freedom of navigation rights

topic.name: Discrimination and Rights Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting individuals from discrimination based on gender identity, sexual orientation, and ensuring privacy rights in educational settings.

Document 1: expands the categories of prohibited discrimination when extending credit to include discrimination on account of sexual orientation or gender identity
prohibited forms of discrimination is actual or perceived.
Document 2: invalidates the Dear Colleague Letter on Transgender Students
guidance to schools receiving federal financial assistance about the treatment of transgender students under the prohibitions against sex discrimination.

topic.name: Telecommunications Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management and transition of telecommunications services and technologies in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the National Telecommunications and Information Administration Organization Act
Requires federal entities to complete spectrum relocation within one year
Document 2: Amends the Digital Television Transition and Public Safety Act of 2005
Requires that coupons for digital-to-analog converter boxes expire six months after issuance
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to make competitive grants to court reporting systems
Promote recruitment, training, and placement of individuals as realtime writers

topic.name: Data Privacy and Protection
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection of personally identifiable information and consumer privacy in the context of data collection and breaches.

Document 1: establish an opt-out list of consumers who have notified the CFPB that they do not wish to allow it to collect personally identifiable information about them
if it experiences a data breach that exposes personally identifiable information about a consumer, to give that consumer one year of free credit monitoring
Document 2: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable protected health information
Document 3: provide individuals who have been affected by a breach of personnel records in a database network with complimentary identity protection coverage
establish a task force to develop specific recommendations on mitigating adverse consequences relating to data breaches

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at the protection and management of wildlife species and their habitats.

Document 1: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973
requires the Secretary of the Interior or the Secretary of Commerce to provide data to affected states
Document 2: amends the Pittman-Robertson Wildlife Restoration Act
extends financial and technical assistance to the states for the promotion of hunting and recreational shooting
Document 3: authorizes payments to a conservation banking program
may result in an adverse impact to one or more species protected under applicable provisions of law

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing support and resources for small businesses through various programs and amendments.

Document 1: This bill amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958 to require the Small Business Administration (SBA) to give priority to small business investment company (SBIC) applications for licenses to operate in a declared major disaster area.

the SBA shall not include the cost basis of any investment made by an SBIC in a small business concern in a major disaster area during the one-year period beginning on the date of the disaster declaration.
Document 2: This bill amends the Small Business Act to require the Small Business Administration (SBA) at least annually to provide to the Defense Acquisition University a list of all changes made in the prior year to regulations promulgated by the SBA that affect federal acquisition.
any materials the SBA has developed to explain, train, or assist federal agencies or departments or small businesses to comply with such regulations.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Labor to establish a program of grants to eligible small business employers to defray the cost of compensation they pay to recent graduates from institutions of higher education.
such graduates to enable them to defray the cost of undertaking further education for up to 24 months in subjects relating to mathematics, science, engineering, or technology.

topic.name: Designation of Public Facilities
topic.description: The documents focus on the formal designation of various public facilities and landmarks in the United States.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 123 11th Avenue South in Nampa, Idaho, as the 'Herbert A Littleton Postal Station.'
Document 2: This bill designates as the Alex Diekmann Peak the 9,765-foot peak located 2.2 miles west-northwest of Finger Mountain on the western boundary of the Lee Metcalf Wilderness in Montana.
Document 3: Designates the U.S. courthouse located at 501 East Court Street in Jackson, Mississippi, as the 'Charles Clark United States Courthouse.'

topic.name: Election Legislation and Reforms
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at regulating and improving the electoral process in the United States.

Document 1: establish the first Saturday and Sunday after the first Friday in November as election days
polling place hours in the United States for both congressional and presidential elections
Document 2: require any person conducting a federal election phone bank to report specified information
limits such requirement to projects contacting at least 1,500 households during the 25 days before a federal election
Document 3: apply the ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nations in connection with elections
applies the ban to domestic corporations with foreign principals

topic.name: Regulatory Compliance and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing regulatory compliance and oversight across various sectors, including finance, trade, and environmental protection.

Document 1: Amends federal money and finance law to repeal certain discretionary exemptions of depository institutions from currency transaction reporting (CTR) requirements
Requires the Secretary to exempt a depository institution from domestic CTR requirements without requiring the depository institution to file a notice of designation of exemption
Document 2: Directs the U.S. Trade Representative (USTR) to report to Congress annually on the estimated annual loss of revenue to holders of U.S. intellectual property rights as a result of intellectual property rights violations in China
Requires the USTR to impose duties on merchandise from China in an amount equivalent to the estimated annual loss of revenue to holders of U.S. intellectual property rights
Document 3: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA) to prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from granting an oil or gas lease to a bidder conducting oil and gas operations off the coast of Cuba
Requires the Secretary to implement an oil spill risk analysis and planning process for oil spill response plans for nondomestic oil spills in the Gulf of Mexico

topic.name: Native American Housing and Rights
topic.description: Legislation focused on the recognition and support of Native American communities and their housing rights.

Document 1: Prohibits the term 'marriage' from including any relationship that the state does not recognize as a marriage.
Defines 'spouse' in the context of state-recognized relationships.
Document 2: Amends the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act to extend housing assistance for Native Americans.
Makes the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands eligible for loan guarantees.
Document 3: Amends the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act to permit Native residents to organize as Urban Corporations.
Authorizes Urban Corporations to establish a settlement trust to promote health, education, and welfare.

topic.name: Consumer Credit Protection for Minors
topic.description: This topic focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing the protection of credit reports for minor consumers, particularly in relation to identity theft and fraud.

Document 1: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act, with respect to protections for credit reports of minor consumers
Prohibits a consumer reporting agency from charging a fee to create, convert, or unblock the file of a consumer who was a victim of fraud or identity theft before his or her 18th birthday.
Document 2: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act, with respect to protections for credit reports of minor consumers
Requires a consumer reporting agency to unblock a blocked file upon request by a guardian or on the minor's 18th birthday.

topic.name: Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to the operations, services, and funding of the United States Postal Service (USPS).

Document 1: removes the authority of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to issue semipostal stamps
the USPS issues semipostals using its own discretion
Document 2: repeals the service standards for market-dominant products implemented by the U.S. Postal Service
directs USPS to reinstate the service standards that were in effect
Document 3: directs the Secretary of Defense to provide for a program under which postal benefits are provided
provides the postal benefits in the form of coupons or other evidence of credit

topic.name: Environmental and Health Compensation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments aimed at providing compensation and support for individuals affected by environmental hazards and health issues related to mining and nuclear exposure.

Document 1: Amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977
repeal the $15 million limitation placed upon the total annual payment to certified states or Indian tribes
Document 2: EEOICPA Ombudsman Extension and Enhancement Act of 2007
assist in making claims for benefits for Department of Energy (DOE) contractor employees who have contracted a covered illness
Document 3: Mohave County Radiation Compensation Act of 2013
include the county of Mohave, Arizona, among affected areas for purposes of payments to individuals with certain medical conditions

topic.name: Commemorative Legislation and National Policies
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts that commemorate individuals or establish national policies related to patriotism and national identity.

Document 1: Establishes the Ronald Reagan Centennial Commission to honor Ronald Reagan
plan and carry out activities to honor Ronald Reagan
Document 2: authorizes U.S. Capitol Police officers to enforce immigration laws
performance of official duties on Capitol Grounds
Document 3: Requires flags to be entirely manufactured in the United States
manufactured in the United States from articles, materials, or supplies entirely grown, produced, or manufactured in the United States

topic.name: Regulatory Compliance in Energy and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses the regulatory frameworks and compliance requirements for safety and integrity in energy production and distribution, particularly focusing on gas pipelines and related operations.

Document 1: Requires each operator of a gas pipeline facility to accelerate the repair, rehabilitation, and replacement of gas piping or equipment that is leaking or may pose high risk of leaking.

Directs the Administrator of the Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration to issue non-binding best practices guidelines for identifying and classifying high-risk pipeline infrastructure.
Document 2: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to require the operator of a drilling and production operation under a lease to submit annually to the Secretary of the Interior a specified certification by the operator's chief executive officer that the operator is in compliance with all pertinent laws and operating regulations.

topic.name: Agricultural and Labor Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to agricultural support and labor rights, focusing on compensation and disaster relief for livestock and fair pay for workers.

Document 1: Family Dairy Preservation Act of 2009
increase milk income loss contract payments
Document 2: livestock compensation program
eligible livestock losses shall include the death of livestock
Document 3: Lilly Ledbetter Fair Pay Act of 2009
unlawful employment practice occurs when a discriminatory compensation decision is adopted

topic.name: Federal Judicial and Legislative Actions
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to the federal judiciary and specific legal jurisdictions in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the federal judicial code to provide for an additional judgeship for the western district of Michigan
increasing the number of judgeships from four to five.
Document 2: Federal Reserve Credit Facility Review Act of 2009
Authorizes the Comptroller General to conduct reviews, including onsite examinations, of any credit facility established by the Federal Reserve Board.
Document 3: Grants U.S. district courts original jurisdiction over any civil action for damages for personal injury or death
no law limiting the jurisdiction of the U.S. courts shall preclude any such action.

topic.name: Support for Military Families and Veterans
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at supporting military families and veterans through various programs and initiatives.

Document 1: Establishes the Commission on Wartime Relocation and Internment of Latin Americans of Japanese descent.
recommend appropriate remedies to Congress based on preliminary findings by the original Commission and new discoveries.
Document 2: Amends the Department of Defense Survivor Benefits Continuing Appropriations Resolution, 2014.
appropriate funds for fiscal years after FY2014 during any period of lapsed appropriations for the payment of death gratuities and related benefits for survivors of deceased servicemembers.
Document 3: Establishes the National Veterans Support Network.
carry out national activities to raise awareness of the challenges facing military families and veterans.

topic.name: Health Policy and Legislation
topic.description: The documents focus on various health-related legislative initiatives aimed at improving medical practices, consumer rights, and awareness in healthcare.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish an advisory council on prostate cancer
Requires the Director of the National Institutes of Health (NIH) to coordinate and intensify research in accordance with the plan developed under this Act
Document 2: This bill directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to prescribe rules prohibiting unfair or deceptive acts or practices in the provision of funeral goods or services
The FTC and states are provided authority to enforce against violations
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish a program to make grants to accredited schools of allopathic or osteopathic medicine
Increase the number of recent allopathic and osteopathic medical school graduates who practice in underserved rural communities

topic.name: Infrastructure Resilience and Efficiency
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing the resilience and efficiency of critical infrastructure systems in response to environmental challenges.

Document 1: establish a program to make grants to state and local governments to assist them in projects that improve the ability of trauma center hospitals and/or airports in hazardous seismic zones to withstand an earthquake
comprehensive strategic earthquake response plan
Document 2: conduct a two-year pilot program to extend the summer pool elevation of 359 feet on such lake
evaluate the effectiveness of such extension and report to Congress
Document 3: require fleet owners or operators to acquire only new fleet vehicles that utilize natural gas as a fuel
further any policy regarding climate change, the control of air pollution, energy independence

topic.name: Government Oversight and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving government efficiency, accountability, and security through oversight and reform initiatives.

Document 1: Establishes the Bipartisan Earmark Reform Commission
report findings, conclusions, and recommendations to the President and Congress.
Document 2: Establishes the Senate Committee to Reduce Government Waste
recommend to the Senate actions designed to modify or eliminate them.
Document 3: Catching Operational Vulnerabilities by Ensuring Random Testing Act of 2009
Requires the Secretary of Homeland Security to make sure that advance notice of a covert test...

topic.name: Legislation Related to Terrorism and Finance
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative acts that address terrorism risk management and financial assistance related to anti-terrorism measures.

Document 1: Amends the Terrorism Risk Insurance Act of 2002 to extend the Terrorism Insurance Program
Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to collect all required terrorism loss risk-spreading premiums
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 and the Palestinian Anti-Terrorism Act of 2006
Revise the presidential certification and related provisions required for U.S. assistance to the Palestinian Authority

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts
topic.description: A collection of U.S. legislative acts addressing various national and global issues including environmental conservation, public health, and economic stability.

Document 3: Declares it is US policy that the principal economic responsibilities of the Government are to establish both long-term economic growth and increases in living standards.
Declares that the promotion of price stability should be the primary long-term goal of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System.

topic.name: Housing Finance Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to housing finance, mortgage origination, and insurance regulations.

Document 1: Amends the National Housing Act to remove the Director of the Enforcement Center from the Mortgagee Review Board
Sets forth mortgagee eligibility criteria.
Document 2: Amends the Truth in Lending Act and the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act of 1974
Defines 'non-profit low-income housing provider' as a tax-exempt charitable organization.
Document 3: Bars from taking effect for a 12-month period any increases in mandatory chargeable risk premium rates for flood insurance coverage
Increases in chargeable risk premium rates shall be made on a yearly basis.

topic.name: Federal Program Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses the establishment and evaluation of federal programs aimed at ensuring accountability and effectiveness in various sectors, including tax benefits and housing finance.

Document 1: IRS program for identity protection for adopted children to prevent tax fraud.
Document 2: Commission on Congressional Budgetary Accountability and Review of Federal Agencies established to assess agency effectiveness.
Document 3: OFHEO's directive to modify growth limitations for Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to address subprime mortgage issues.

topic.name: Regulatory Oversight and Agency Actions
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the oversight of regulatory actions, agency accountability, and the processes governing consent decrees and settlement agreements.

Document 1: Defines a 'covered civil action' as a civil action seeking to compel agency action and alleging that an agency is unlawfully withholding or unreasonably delaying an agency action
Requires an agency against which a covered civil action is brought to publish the notice of intent to sue and the complaint in a readily accessible manner
Document 2: Amends the Federal Housing Enterprises Financial Safety and Soundness Act of 1992 to authorize the Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA), as a receiver of the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac), to revoke the charters of such enterprises
Document 3: Directs the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) to establish an application process under which a person who lives or does business in a state may apply to have an area in the state identified as a rural area

topic.name: Legal Reforms and Protections
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing legal protections for individuals in various contexts, including law enforcement practices and employment discrimination.

Document 1: Effective Law Enforcement Through Transparent Interrogations Act of 2010
render inadmissible as evidence in the prosecution of a federal felony all prior and subsequent statements by an individual during a custodial interrogation that are not electronically recorded
Document 2: Telephone Records Protection Act

Repeals a provision of the federal criminal code requiring a provider of electronic communication service or remote computing service to disclose to a governmental entity local and long distance telephone connection records
Document 3: Protecting Older Workers Against Discrimination Act

amends the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 to specify that an unlawful employment practice is established when the complaining party demonstrates that age or participation in investigations was a motivating factor

topic.name: Government Policy and Oversight
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to government operations, transparency, and the facilitation of services in various sectors.

Document 1: This bill amends the Homeland Security Act of 2002 to authorize the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) to enter into agreements with certain entities for the CBP to provide customs, agricultural processing, border security, or inspection-related immigration services.
CBP and the Government Accountability Office shall report annually to specified congressional committees on CBP fee agreements and donations.
Document 2: Amends part D (Child Support and Establishment of Paternity) of title IV of the Social Security Act to authorize the Secretary of State to waive certain travel restrictions for an individual otherwise subject to them.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to make available on a publicly-available Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) website: (1) data files that contain information on VA research.

Requires the Department of Veterans Affairs-Department of Defense Joint Executive to submit to the Secretary and the Secretary of Defense (DOD) options and recommendations for establishing a program of long-term cooperation and data-sharing.

topic.name: Veterans Education and Workforce Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and programs aimed at enhancing educational opportunities and workforce readiness for veterans and disadvantaged youth.

Document 1: Increases the amount of educational assistance payments made by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs
pursuing an apprenticeship or on-job training
Document 2: establish and carry out a scholarship program of financial assistance
enrolled in a program of study leading to a degree or certificate in visual impairment
Document 3: award competitive, renewable, five-year grants to eligible partnerships
provide disadvantaged youth with the support needed to graduate from secondary school

topic.name: Legislation on Waterways and Navigation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating waterways, protecting aquatic ecosystems, and enhancing navigation safety.

Document 1: Prohibit the importation or shipment of the species of mussels of the genus Dreissena
Protecting Lakes Against Quaggas Act of 2013
Document 2: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930
Waive the requirement that Canadian vessels report immediately to the nearest customs facility
Document 3: Adjusts the boundary of the Apostle Islands National Lakeshore
Authorize the Secretary to operate, maintain, keep, locate, inspect, repair, and replace any federal aid to navigation

topic.name: Government Financial Accountability
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing transparency, accountability, and performance measurement in government financial practices and foreign assistance programs.

Document 1: Requires the head of each federal agency to prepare for a fiscal year and submit to the Comptroller General (GAO) three quarterly and one annual consolidated financial statement based on the fair-value accrual accounting method.
Requires the Comptroller General to audit the consolidated financial statements prepared by each federal agency for each fiscal year.
Document 2: Directs the Comptroller General to include in the annual report to Congress on the Government Accountability Office (GAO) a list of the most common grounds for sustaining protests relating to bids for contracts during the year.
Document 3: Directs the President to establish guidelines for the establishment of measurable goals, performance metrics, and monitoring and evaluation plans for U.S. foreign assistance programs.

topic.name: Land Conservation and Child Protection
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at land conservation in specific regions and the establishment of training programs for child protection professionals.

Document 1: amends the Highlands Conservation Act to reauthorize land conservation partnership projects
assist states, units of local government, and private forest and farm landowners in the conservation of land and natural resources
Document 2: Directs the Attorney General to establish a program to sustain regional training centers
develop model interdisciplinary undergraduate curricula on recognizing and responding to cases of child maltreatment
